hero.question: Will Duke win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 17.5%, the market is pricing Duke as a tier-two contender with roughly 1-in-6 odds, which feels about right for a program that consistently recruits elite talent but hasn't won since 2010. The real tell is yesterday's 1-point drop suggests smart money is rotating out on injury concerns or Q3 roster clarity, so wait for lineup confirmations before fading the crowd here.

daily_take.headline: Duke's Tournament Odds Cracking. Smart Money Knows Why.
daily_take.deck: Duke dropped 100 bps overnight to 17.5%, a minor move that signals real concern. The market is repricing risk around roster stability and next year's recruiting class. Arizona jumped 220 bps to 18.5% in the same window, suggesting Duke money is rotating to the better value. At 17.5%, Duke feels fairly priced now, maybe even tight for a blue-blood program with a 2026 title window still open, but the directional move matters more than the absolute level.
